Reference

Scoring.

How the daily ranking, ratios, and tournament points are computed. The maths behind the leaderboard.

The Scoring System
Simple Score — How Points Are Awarded
Each day, all players who submit a result are ranked by their solve time — fastest to slowest.

Points are awarded as follows:
🥇 Fastest → N points (N = number of players that day)
🥈 2nd → N−1 points
🥉 3rd → N−2 points
🐌 Slowest → 1 point

If you don't submit, you receive 0 points for that day.
Example Day (6 players)
Rank Player Time Points
🥇 1stIgor0:076
🥈 2ndBorg0:115
🥉 3rdAdam0:154
4thAnna0:223
5thLinda0:302
LastKaissar1:041
Draws — Tied Times
When two or more players finish in the same time, they are awarded the same rank ex aequo and receive the same points.

The next player below the tied group is ranked as if the tied players each occupied a separate slot — so their rank, and points, are reduced accordingly.

Example: Borg and Bazyli both finish in 0:05 out of 4 players.
They both take 1st place → 4 pts.
The next player takes 3rd place → 2 pts (rank 2 is skipped).
Last place still receives 1 pt.
Example Day (4 players, 2-way draw)
Rank Player Time Points
🥇 1stBorg0:054
🥇 1stBazyli0:054
(2nd skipped)
3rdAdam0:062
LastIgor0:081
Ratio Score — Benchmark: Fastest
The fastest player each day scores 1.00 (the benchmark).
Every other player's score = their time ÷ fastest time.
Players who don't submit receive their own average ratio for that day of the week as a penalty.

Lower total = better. The leaderboard shows each player's average ratio per weekday. 🥇 Days = days you were the fastest.
Example Day (3 players, fastest = 0:30)
Player Time ÷ 0:30 Ratio
🥇 Igor0:3030÷301.00
Borg0:4545÷301.50
Anna1:0060÷302.00
Linda (no submit)—penaltyown avg
Median Ratio — Benchmark: Day's Median Time
The day's median time (middle value when sorted) scores 1.00.
Every player's score = their time ÷ median time.
Ratio < 1.00 = faster than half the field. Ratio > 1.00 = slower than half the field.
Players who don't submit receive their own average median-ratio for that day of the week as a penalty.

≤ Median Days counts days where your ratio ≤ 1.00 (you beat the median). The leaderboard shows your average ratio per weekday, coloured green if ≤ 1.00, red if above.
Example Day (4 players, median = 0:45)
Player Time ÷ 0:45 Ratio
Igor0:3030÷450.67 ✓
Borg0:4040÷450.89 ✓
Anna0:5050÷451.11
Adam1:0060÷451.33
Median of [30, 40, 50, 60] = (40+50)/2 = 45s
Average Ratio — Benchmark: Day's Mean Time
The day's mean time (sum ÷ number of players) scores 1.00.
Every player's score = their time ÷ mean time.
Ratio < 1.00 = faster than average. Ratio > 1.00 = slower than average.
Players who don't submit receive their own average mean-ratio for that day of the week as a penalty.

≤ Avg Days counts days where your ratio ≤ 1.00 (you beat the mean). The leaderboard shows your average ratio per weekday, coloured green if ≤ 1.00, red if above.

Note: unlike the median, the mean is sensitive to outliers — one very slow time pulls the benchmark up, making it easier to beat.
Example Day (4 players, mean = 0:45)
Player Time ÷ 0:45 Ratio
Igor0:2020÷450.44 ✓
Borg0:4040÷450.89 ✓
Anna0:5555÷451.22
Adam1:0565÷451.44
Mean of [20, 40, 55, 65] = 180÷4 = 45s
Date mapping: Each game number is mapped to a fixed calendar date — e.g. Queens #611 = 1 Jan 2026, Zip #290 = 1 Jan 2026, etc. Submissions sent after midnight are corrected to their proper puzzle date.
Data Downloads
Raw JSON data files parsed from the WhatsApp chats — all dates corrected and points computed.

The handmade trophy archive

Champions past

Every month, each game's winner receives a small painting from the previous champion — a one-of-a-kind canvas crowned with their name. Browse the archive of glory below.

How tournament scoring works
Who qualifies? Players who submit on at least 50% of days elapsed in the month qualify. Once the month is complete, the full calendar length is used.

Daily points Only the qualifying participants count for the tournament. If N players qualify, the daily ranking works exactly like Simple Score among those N players: 1st gets N pts, 2nd gets N−1, …, last gets 1.

When someone doesn't submit Non-submitters receive 0 points for that day. The point allocation for those who did submit is unchanged — the last submitter still receives the points for that finishing position.

Draws Tied players share the same rank and receive the same points. The next distinct rank skips as many slots as the tied group occupied.
Example — 5 participants, 1 missing
Rank Player Time Points
🥇 1stIgor0:075
2ndAnna0:114
3rdBorg0:193
4thAdam0:342
—Kaissarno submit0
Position 5 (1 pt) is unawarded — Adam gets 2 pts, not 1.
